title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Brow… · Inference Brief: Configuration Secret Sources
summary: A published TypeScript SDK needs typed operation boundaries, an injected fetch function, ordered wrappers, runtime validators, and environment-safe configuration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts configuration secret sources increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate environment, file, runtime, and secret-provider configuration with precedence, rotation, and safe diagnostics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Expose small operation methods with typed inputs, trusted outputs, stable failures, and hidden protocol plumbing. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
